title: corynotheca licrota
intro: Corynotheca licrota, also known as the 'sticky flower' due to its sticky or resinous flowers, is a plant species native to Australia. The Corynotheca licrota plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and infections. It is believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing and soothing irritated skin. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of infusions or tinctures, have been used to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
